channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: LinkedIn's Editor-in-Chief Talks Content Monetization and AI's Role in the Job Market
item.description: The article features an interview between Sara Goo, President of Washington Post Creator, and Dan Roth, Editor-in-Chief and VP of Content at LinkedIn. They discuss LinkedIn's recent announcements aime...

item.title: Benhur Abalos Appointed as Marcos' First Cabinet Secretary
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has appointed Benhur Abalos, his former Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) chief, as his first Cabinet secretary. The appointment was announced by Pa...

item.title: Edgars Expands with New Stores Following Turnaround Strategy
item.description: Edgars, the South African retail brand, is set to open 50 new stores over the next two years as part of its turnaround strategy. This marks a significant shift from previous years of reducing its reta...

item.title: 75-Year-Old Widow in Kakamega Cares for Three Adult Sons with Mental Illness
item.description: Gladys Okali, a 75-year-old widow from Emuruba village in western Kenya, has spent over 25 years caring for her three sons who live with mental illnesses. The community has labeled her home as 'the ho...

item.title: Tanzanian football legend Samatta announces retirement from international career
item.description: Mbwana Ally Samatta, 33, has officially retired from international football after a 15-year career with Tanzania's national team, Taifa Stars. The striker, who captained the team for nearly a decade, ...

item.title: Modi's 12-Year Heritage Conservation Efforts Strengthen Asia Ties
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government has undertaken extensive heritage conservation projects across Asia over the past 12 years, using shared civilisational ties to bolster diplomatic relations. ...

item.title: NATO Allies Commit £37bn to New Long-Range Missile Project
item.description: Twelve NATO countries, including the UK, have pledged over £37bn ($50bn) over the next decade for the Deep Precision Strike missile project, aimed at enhancing Europe's defense capabilities. The initi...

item.title: Public health agency tests 75+ local lakes for E. coli contamination
item.description: The Simcoe Muskoka District Health Unit (SMDHU) is conducting extensive water quality testing across over 75 lakes in the region to assess potential E. coli contamination. This initiative follows grow...

item.title: Women's Voluntary Service Mobile Canteens During WWII Provided Essential Support to Civilians Amid Bombings
item.description: During World War II's Blitz (1940–1941), the Women’s Voluntary Service (WVS) operated mobile canteens known as 'Blitz Canteens' to support civilians and emergency workers in bombed areas of London. Th...

item.title: SHA Introduces Fingerprint Verification for Children to Fight Health Insurance Fraud
item.description: The Social Health Authority (SHA) has rolled out biometric fingerprint verification for children aged 7-17 to prevent fraud in Kenya's public health insurance system. This initiative, part of broader ...

item.title: Incident Between PAL Plane and Saudia Jet at NAIA
item.description: A parked Philippine Airlines (PAL) aircraft suffered damage to its tail fin after a collision with a Saudia Airways jet at Ninoy Aquino International Airport (NAIA). The incident occurred on July 8, 2...

item.title: canberra police appeal for info on missing man mika sutinen
item.description: Police in Canberra are urgently seeking public assistance to locate 46-year-old Mika Sutinen, who has been missing since June 25. Last seen in Belconnen, Sutinen is described as Caucasian with an oliv...

item.title: Cross-Border Crackdown Targets India-Based Organized Crime Groups in Major Arrests
item.description: Canadian authorities have charged 37 individuals linked to three prominent organized crime groups, including the Bishnoi gang, in a sweeping operation targeting transnational criminal networks. The ar...

item.title: US and Iran escalate tensions with military strikes over Strait of Hormuz attacks
item.description: The United States conducted military strikes against Iran in response to alleged attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical maritime route for global oil trade. The US military ...

item.title: Investing tax refunds in tax-free accounts for long-term growth
item.description: The article explains how tax refunds, often perceived as 'found money', should be strategically invested in tax-free savings accounts (TFSAs) to maximize returns. It highlights the psychological conce...

item.title: Pasadena Doctor's Attempted Murder Charges Dismissed After Mental Health Diversion Program
item.description: Dharmesh Patel, a respected radiologist in Pasadena, was accused of attempting to kill his wife and two children by driving his Tesla off a 250-foot cliff in Northern California in January 2023. Prose...

item.title: International Operation Targets India-Based Organized Crime Syndicates
item.description: An international law enforcement operation has led to the arrest of 24 individuals linked to India-based organized crime networks. The operation, involving US, Canadian, and European authorities, targ...

item.title: EFCC's Asset Recovery Boosts Education in Nigeria
item.description: Minister of Education, Maruf Olatunji Alausa, praised EFCC Chairman Ola Olukoyede for transforming the commission into a model for anti-corruption efforts under President Tinubu. During a visit to EFC...

item.title: BRICS Nations Commit to Combating Illicit Drug Trafficking via Guwahati Declaration
item.description: The BRICS nations have adopted the Guwahati Declaration, reaffirming their commitment to coordinated efforts against illicit drug trafficking. This declaration, adopted during a two-day meeting of ant...

item.title: Switzerland reach first World Cup quarter-final since 1954 as Argentina complete dramatic comeback against Egypt
item.description: Switzerland and Argentina have secured their places in the World Cup quarter-finals after contrasting but equally memorable last-16 victories. Switzerland progressed by defeating Colombia 4-3 in a pen...
